reasoning: The 12-month percentage change in the US Consumer Price Index (CPI) has remained well below 4.1% throughout the first 9 months of 2025. September 2025 data, as reported by Reuters just prior to the cutoff date, showed the 12-month CPI at 3.0%, following 2.9% in August. The highest reported year-over-year rate in early 2025 appears to have been around 3.3%. Given that only October, November, and December remain, pushing the year-over-year inflation rate to 4.1% or higher would require an extreme, sudden surge in monthly inflation. Monthly changes have recently hovered around 0.1% to 0.4%, so the required spike of over 1.1% in the year-over-year rate in just one quarter is practically impossible barring a catastrophic economic shock. Therefore, the probability of reaching 4.1% YoY in 2025 is estimated to be at the minimum allowed threshold.
